name: Maple-Glazed Shallots
recipeInstructions:
1. Leave the shallots whole. If some are much larger than others, remove outer layers to reveal twin inner shallots of a more equal size.
2. Melt butter in a heavy pan large enough to accommodate the shallots in a single, snug layer.
3. Fry the shallots until they develop a few brown patches on the surface—about 5 minutes.
4. Drizzle over maple syrup and stir for about 1 minute.
5. Add lemon juice, salt, pepper, and just enough water to cover. Bring to a boil.
6. Reduce heat and simmer gently, stirring occasionally, until the shallots are very tender (add more water if necessary) and there is only a thin layer of syrup left in the pan—approximately 30 to 40 minutes.
7. Reheat when needed. Stored covered in the refrigerator for up to 2–3 days.
